lessonTitle: New and Emerging SCD Therapies — An Overview

questions.question: My knowledge about Latest Innovations in Sickle Cell Disease on a scale of 1 to 10 is ___ .
My comfort level with Latest Innovations in SCD on a scale of 1 to 10 is ___ .
My confidence level with Latest Innovations in SCD on a scale of 1 to 10 is ___ .

items.html: Review Crizanlizumab and its role in SCD management
Summarize new and emerging therapies for SCD
Identify how these therapies work and current evidence

Dr. Sarah Patterson: Advisory boards — Novartis Canada, Pfizer Canada, Global Blood Therapeutics Canada, Forma Therapeutics (now Novo Nordisk); Speaker's Bureau — Novartis Canada, Pfizer Canada; Research Grant — Novartis Canada
Dr. Seethal Jacob: No disclosures
Karen Fleming: No disclosures

Dr. Kevin Kuo: Advisory boards — Novartis Canada, Pfizer Canada, Global Blood Therapeutics Canada, bluebird bio; Research Grant — Novartis Canada, Pfizer Canada, Global Blood Therapeutics Canada, Forma Therapeutics

data.bubble_text: Instead of focusing on treating the pain, let's look at innovations that are focused on targeting the pathobiology of SCD to prevent pain/complications.

items.title: Mechanism
items.html:
• Humanized monoclonal antibody
  • Targets P-Selectin on activated endothelium
  • Blocks the binding of P-selectin to its ligands (PSGL-1)
  • Reduces cell-cell interactions between sickle red blood cells, platelets, endothelial cells and white blood cells
  • These interactions are thought to lead to vascular occlusion

items.title: Evidence: SUSTAIN Trial (Phase II)
items.html:
• 198 patients with SCD aged 16 and older
  • >2 VOCs in previous 12 months, unless transfused >10 times in the past year
  • HU allowed
  • Compared 5mg/kg and 2.5 mg/kg Crizanlizumab vs Placebo for 52 weeks

items.title: Results
items.html:
• 5mg/kg demonstrated a 45.3% lower median annual rate of VOC vs placebo (p=0.01)
  • 5mg/kg significantly prolonged time to first VOC (p=0.001)
  • 5mg/kg significantly prolonged time to second VOC (p=0.02)
  • 5mg/kg: 35.8% of patients had no VOC vs 17% placebo (p=0.02)

items.title: Health Canada Approval
items.html:
• Crizanlizumab is approved by Health Canada for SCD patients 16 years and older with VOCs
  • Dosing: 5 mg/kg IV Infusion at Week 0, 2 and every 4 weeks thereafter

items.title: L-Glutamine
items.html:
Mechanism:

  • Reduces oxidative stress by synthesizing NADPH and glutathione

Evidence:

  • 2 RCTs - Phase II and Phase III, placebo controlled
  • Phase III: Lower cumulative pain crises at 48 weeks

items.title: Canakinumab
items.html:
Mechanism:

  • Selective blockade of IL-1b to reduce inflammation

Evidence:

  • Phase II placebo controlled randomized control trial of 49 participants
  • Reduced hemolysis, number and duration of hospitalizations

items.title: Crovalimab
items.html:
Mechanism:

  • Complement blockade (C5) which reduces ongoing inflammation in the setting of VOC/endothelial activation/hemolysis

Evidence:

  • Phase Ib Crosswalk-A trial in progress
  • Subcutaneous injection

items.title: Voxelotor
items.html:
Mechanism:

  • Hemoglobin S polymerization inhibitor, increased oxygen affinity resulting in increased red cell survival and reduced hemolysis

Evidence:

  • HOPE trial (Phase III)
  • Hb increased by at least 10 points in the high dose (89%) vs placebo (25%) at 72 weeks

items.title: Decitabine and Tetrahydrouridine
items.html:
Mechanism:

  • DNMT1 is the enzyme that silences HbF from infancy onwards
  • Decitabine interferes with the function of DNMT1
  • Tetrahydrouridine inhibits enzyme that rapidly deactivates Decitabine

Evidence:

  • Phase 1 trial
  • Increased Hb 12-19 g/L
  • HbF increased by 4-9%

items.title: GBT021601
items.html:
Mechanism:

  • GBT021601 stabilizes hemoglobin (Hb) in the oxygenated state to inhibit HbS polymerization
  • Longer half-life than voxelotor and potential to achieve higher Hb occupancies

Evidence:

  • Phase 2 Part A dose-finding study
  • Mean increase in Hb from baseline was 2.67g/dL for the 100-mg group and 3.17g/dL for the 150-mg group

items.title: Mitapivat
items.html:
Mechanism:

  • Activator of pyruvate kinase (involved in RBC metabolism)
  • Increases adenosine triphosphate (ATP) which improves RBC survival
  • Decreases 2,3 diphosphoglycerate (2, 3-DPG) which increases oxygen affinity of Hb

Evidence:

  • Phase 2 double-blind trial
  • Hb response in 46.2% of 50mg arm and 50% of 100mg arm

items.title: dWIZ-2
items.html:
Mechanism:

  • Targeted Degradation of the Wiz Transcription Factor for Gamma Globin De-Repression
  • Cereblon (CRBN) is a naturally existing protein degradation machinery in cells
  • dWIZ-2 degrades WIZ and induces HbF in CD34+ derived erythroblasts

Evidence:

  • CD34+ derived erythroblasts from sickle cell disease donors and mouse xenotransplantation model

items.title: AB1
items.html:
Mechanism:

  • AB1 is a potent non-cytotoxic agent which depletes DNMT1 to induce HbF in sickle erythroid progenitors (Li et al., unpublished data)

Evidence:

  • In SCD Townes mice model, AB1 increases γ-globin mRNA and F-cells, decreases sickle cell phenotype in blood, improves splenic hematopoiesis

data.bubble_text: I want to share some new updates with you regarding the SUSTAIN trial for Crizanlizumab.

items.title: STAND Trial
items.html:
• Pivotal Phase III study
  • 5 mg/kg Crizanlizumab vs placebo (2:1 ratio)
  • 6 to <18 years of age (pediatric population only)
  • Primary endpoint: Annual rate of Sickle Cell-related Pain Crises (SCPCs) over 12 months

items.title: STAND Results (Inusa et al., 2023)
items.html:
• Primary endpoint not met
  • Mean annual SCPC rate, 2.00 in Crizanlizumab arm vs 1.99 in Placebo arm (p=0.7)
  • Adverse events and Serious Adverse Events were similar in both groups

items.title: Update
items.html: • Novartis voluntarily withdrew Crizanlizumab from global markets in June 2024, following these results in pediatric patients and results of the Phase III NIAID-sponsored REDS-IV-P SCD-SAINT trial in adult patients

data.html: Let's split the options into curative and non-curative therapies. We will cover curative therapies in detail in Lesson 3.

Starting with non-curative options, recall the pathobiology of SCD: With the Hb S mutation, the hemoglobin becomes deoxygenated. There is increased hydrophobicity and the hemoglobin polymerizes (becomes sticky), causing the red blood cells to become sickled. Options to interfere with the sickle process include: a) improving oxygen affinity of Hb b) Increasing Fetal Hemoglobin (HbF)

HbS Polymerization can be affected by: 1. Hemoglobin oxygen affinity, 2. Hemoglobin concentration, 3. Delay time, 4. Other (hyperviscosity, temperature)

Increasing HbF is the target of gene therapies because HbF does not polymerize and can actually prevent polymerization of HbS. In general, >20-30% HbF levels are required to prevent sickling.

Photo credit: Salinas Cisneros G & Thein SL (2020)

data.question: True or False: Hydroxyurea is the most proven and effective therapy for SCD.
data.feedback_correct: True! Hydroxyurea remains the most widely used therapy for SCD. Hydroxyurea increases HbF, decreases VOC, acute chest syndrome, transfusion requirements, and pain. However, it requires tailored dosing and hematologic monitoring.

items.html: Crizanlizumab has been voluntarily withdrawn from markets due to failure of STAND trial and NIAID-sponsored REDS-IV-P SCD-SAINT trial
Many promising new therapies are targeting the pathobiology of SCD
Hydroxyurea remains the most proven and effective therapy for SCD
